peerBenchmarks.companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa

bestPractices.title: King IV Board Evaluation Framework
bestPractices.description: Annual independent board performance evaluation aligned to King IV Report on Corporate Governance
bestPractices.implementationGuide: Engage external consultants for annual board review against King IV principles
bestPractices.expectedBenefits: Improved board effectiveness, accountability and stakeholder confidence

bestPractices.title: PFMA Compliance Framework
bestPractices.description: Structured compliance with Public Finance Management Act requirements for SOEs
bestPractices.implementationGuide: Implement PFMA compliance calendar, quarterly reporting to shareholder ministry
bestPractices.expectedBenefits: Reduced audit findings, improved AGSA outcomes

bestPractices.title: Predictive Maintenance Programme
bestPractices.description: AI-driven asset maintenance optimisation for power generation and rail assets
bestPractices.implementationGuide: Deploy IoT sensors and machine learning for failure prediction

bestPractices.title: Just Energy Transition Partnership
bestPractices.description: Structured transition from coal to renewable energy aligned to Paris Agreement commitments
bestPractices.implementationGuide: Develop JET implementation plan, access JETP funding for coal community transition
bestPractices.expectedBenefits: Climate leadership, access to concessional finance, reduced carbon liability

rankings.rationale: Government debt relief package and improved operational performance
Operational underperformance, high debt levels and governance concerns

rankings.companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
rankings.rationale: Improved carbon management and community investment programmes
Improved generation performance and renewable energy integration

gapAnalyses.gap: Negative equity and high debt burden
gapAnalyses.rootCause: Medupi/Kusile cost overruns, tariff below cost-reflective levels, state capture losses
gapAnalyses.recommendedActions: Debt relief programme, cost-reflective tariffs, operational efficiency

gapAnalyses.gap: Low locomotive and port equipment availability
gapAnalyses.rootCause: State capture procurement irregularities, deferred maintenance
gapAnalyses.recommendedActions: Locomotive maintenance programme, crane replacement, PSP reforms

gapAnalyses.companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
gapAnalyses.gap: Massive rolling stock and infrastructure deficit
gapAnalyses.currentState: 25% fleet availability, 40% infrastructure in poor condition
gapAnalyses.targetState: 80% fleet availability, infrastructure restored to safe operating standard
gapAnalyses.rootCause: Vandalism, cable theft, deferred maintenance, state capture in procurement
gapAnalyses.recommendedActions: Fleet modernisation programme, fibre optic signalling, security deployment
